title: Are Women Really Better at Multitasking Than Men?
gist: A new Psychological Research study using a simulated cooking-and-phone-number multitasking test on 41 men and 37 women in London challenges the popular belief that women are inherently better multitaskers than men, though the article itself flags at least one exception.

insights.claim: A widespread belief holds that women are superior multitaskers, commonly attributed to their historically greater share of childcare responsibilities, which demands frequent task-switching.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Identifies the conventional wisdom the new study is designed to test, a useful baseline before citing the counter-finding.
insights.locator: That may be one reason that women

insights.claim: A new study published in Psychological Research finds that men perform just as well as women on multitasking tasks, contradicting the common assumption of a female multitasking advantage.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: A popular belief frequently invoked in workplace and parenting narratives may not hold up empirically; worth flagging when it appears as an unexamined premise elsewhere.
insights.locator: But according to a new study in Psychological Research

insights.claim: London-based psychologists tested multitasking ability in 41 men and 37 women using a simulated kitchen task combined with an interrupting phone-number recall and number-letter matching assignment.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Documents the study's methodology and sample size, useful context for weighing how much confidence to place in the finding.
insights.locator: To test their respective multitasking capabilities

insights.claim: The article itself signals that the men-women multitasking parity finding still has at least one notable exception, implying the result is not a clean, uniform reversal of the conventional wisdom that women multitask better.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Treat the 'men are equally good at multitasking' headline as incomplete until the specific exception is identified in the full study.
insights.quote: There’s at least one glaring exception
insights.locator: There’s at least one glaring exception
